title: Librarians
description: Administer and maintain libraries or collections of information, for public or private access through reference or borrowing. Work in a variety of settings, such as educational institutions, museums, and corporations, and with various types of informational materials, such as books, periodicals, recordings, films, and databases. Tasks may include acquiring, cataloging, and circulating library materials, and user services such as locating and organizing information, providing instruction on how to access information, and setting up and operating a library's media equipment.

tasks.task: Check books in and out of the library.
Teach library patrons basic computer skills, such as searching computerized databases.
Maintain hardware, software, and audio-visual equipment, including computers, media equipment, scanners, color copiers, and color laser printers.
Assemble and arrange display materials.
Set up, adjust, and operate audio-visual equipment for meetings, events, classes, seminars, and video conferences.
Represent the library or institution on internal and external committees.
Arrange for interlibrary loans of materials not available in a particular library.
Evaluate vendor products and performance, negotiate contracts, and place orders.
Confer with teachers to select course materials and determine training aids best suited to particular grade levels.
Compile lists of books, periodicals, articles, and audio-visual materials on particular subjects.
Engage in professional development activities, such as continuing education classes and attending conferences, workshops, and professional meetings.
Develop, maintain, and troubleshoot information access aids such as databases, annotated bibliographies, and online tutorials.
Evaluate materials to determine outdated or unused items for discard.
Direct and train library staff in duties such as cataloging, shelving, and library operations.
Develop library policies and procedures.
Locate unusual or unique information in response to specific requests.
Plan and deliver client-centered programs and services, such as storytelling for children and newsletters.
Explain the use of library facilities, resources, and services, and provide information about library policies.
Respond to customer complaints, taking action as necessary.
Code, classify, and catalog library materials based on subject matter or standard library classification systems.
Confer with colleagues and community members to conduct informational programs and make collection decisions.
Plan and teach classes on information literacy, library instruction, and technology use.
Supervise daily library operations, budgeting, planning, and personnel activities.
Analyze patrons' requests to determine needed information and assist in locating that information.
Maintain up-to-date records of circulation and materials, correct cataloging errors, and maintain inventory.
Review and evaluate materials using book reviews, catalogs, and faculty recommendations to select and order resources.

tools_used.name: Liquid crystal display LCD projection systems

Develop policies or procedures for archives, museums or libraries.
Plan community programs or activities for the general public.
Construct exhibits or parts of exhibits.
Operate audiovisual equipment.
Maintain computer equipment or software.
Maintain operational records.
Maintain inventory records.

Collaborate with other teaching professionals to develop educational programs.
Negotiate purchases or contracts.
Train staff members.
Direct activities of subordinates.
Serve on institutional or departmental committees.
Direct department activities.
Classify materials according to standard systems.
Process library materials.
Maintain inventories of materials, equipment, or products.
Maintain the inventory of equipment.
Inventory materials or equipment.
